# LivePort

> Secure localhost tunnels for AI agents — expose local servers to the internet with bridge key authentication

## Quick Start

```bash
# Install CLI
npm install -g @liveport/cli

# Create tunnel (requires bridge key from https://liveport.dev/keys)
liveport connect 3000 --key lpk_your_bridge_key_here

# Or use env var
export LIVEPORT_KEY=lpk_your_bridge_key_here
liveport connect 3000
```

## CLI Commands

```
liveport connect <port>          Create tunnel to expose local port
  -k, --key <key>                Bridge key for auth (or LIVEPORT_KEY env)
  -s, --server <url>             Tunnel server URL (default: https://tunnel.liveport.online)
  -r, --region <region>          Server region

liveport status                  Show current tunnel status
liveport disconnect              Disconnect active tunnel
  -a, --all                      Disconnect all tunnels

liveport config set <key> <val>  Set config value (key, server)
liveport config get <key>        Get config value
liveport config list             List all config values
liveport config delete <key>     Delete config value
```

## Agent SDK

```typescript
import { LivePortAgent } from '@liveport/agent-sdk';

const agent = new LivePortAgent({
  key: process.env.LIVEPORT_BRIDGE_KEY!,
  apiUrl: 'https://liveport.dev',          // optional
  tunnelUrl: 'https://tunnel.liveport.online', // optional, for connect()
  timeout: 30000,                           // optional
});

// Option A: Agent creates tunnel directly (no CLI needed)
const tunnelA = await agent.connect(3000);
await agent.waitForReady(tunnelA);         // polls until reachable end-to-end
// Run tests/workflows against tunnelA.url
await agent.disconnect();

// Option B: Wait for developer-created tunnel (CLI creates it)
const tunnelB = await agent.waitForTunnel({ timeout: 30000, pollInterval: 1000 });
console.log(tunnelB.url);  // https://abc123.liveport.online

// List tunnels
const tunnels = await agent.listTunnels();

// Cleanup
await agent.disconnect();
```

## Agent SDK Types

```typescript
interface LivePortAgentConfig {
  key: string;         // Bridge key (required)
  apiUrl?: string;     // API base URL (default: https://liveport.dev)
  tunnelUrl?: string;  // Tunnel server URL for connect() (default: https://tunnel.liveport.online)
  timeout?: number;    // Default timeout ms (default: 30000)
}

interface ConnectOptions {
  serverUrl?: string;  // Override tunnel server URL (same server as config.tunnelUrl)
  timeout?: number;    // Connection timeout ms
}

interface WaitForTunnelOptions {
  timeout?: number;       // Timeout ms (default: 30000)
  pollInterval?: number;  // Poll interval ms (default: 1000)
}

interface WaitForReadyOptions {
  timeout?: number;       // Timeout ms (default: 30000)
  pollInterval?: number;  // Poll interval ms (default: 1000)
  healthPath?: string;    // Health check path (default: "/")
}

interface AgentTunnel {
  tunnelId: string;
  subdomain: string;
  url: string;
  localPort: number;
  createdAt: Date;
  expiresAt: Date;
}

class TunnelTimeoutError extends Error { }
class ApiError extends Error {
  statusCode: number;
  code: string;
}
class ConnectionError extends Error { }
```

## Bridge Keys

- Format: `lpk_<32 chars>` (36 total characters)
- Auth: Pass via `--key` flag, `LIVEPORT_KEY` env var, or `liveport config set key`
- Manage at: https://liveport.dev/keys

## WebSocket Protocol (CLI/SDK ↔ Tunnel Server)

Connection: `WSS /connect` with headers `X-Bridge-Key`, `X-Local-Port`

### Message Types

| Type | Direction | Description |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| `connected` | Server→Client | Tunnel established (tunnelId, subdomain, url, expiresAt) |
| `error` | Server→Client | Error (code, message, fatal, retryAfter?) |
| `heartbeat` | CLI→Server (CLI mode); Server→Agent (SDK mode) | CLI sends heartbeat every 10s; server sends heartbeat to SDK agents |
| `heartbeat_ack` | Server→CLI (CLI mode); Agent→Server (SDK mode) | Heartbeat acknowledgment response |
| `http_request` | Server→Client | Forward HTTP request (method, path, headers, body base64) |
| `http_response` | Client→Server | Return HTTP response (status, headers, body base64) |
| `websocket_upgrade` | Server→Client | WebSocket upgrade request (path, headers, subprotocol) |
| `websocket_upgrade_response` | Client→Server | Accept/reject upgrade |
| `websocket_data` | Bidirectional | WebSocket message relay (data base64, binary flag) |
| `websocket_close` | Bidirectional | Close WebSocket (code, reason, initiator) |
| `disconnect` | Bidirectional | Graceful disconnect |

## Tunnel Server HTTP Endpoints

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/health` | Server health check (connections, uptime) |
| GET | `/api/tls-check?domain=` | On-demand TLS cert validation |
| ALL | `*` | Catch-all: proxies to tunnel by subdomain |

## Limits

- Free tier: 1 concurrent tunnel
- Paid tiers: Up to 5 concurrent tunnels per key
- Max request body: 10MB
- Max WebSocket frame: 10MB
- Request timeout: 30s
- Heartbeat: 10s interval, 30s timeout

## Shared Types

```typescript
// Bridge key format
type BridgeKey = {
  id: string; userId: string; name: string;
  keyHash: string; keyPrefix: string;
  status: 'active' | 'revoked' | 'expired';
  expiresAt?: Date; maxUses?: number; currentUses: number;
  allowedPort?: number;
}

// Tunnel
type Tunnel = {
  id: string; userId: string; bridgeKeyId?: string;
  subdomain: string; name?: string; localPort: number;
  publicUrl: string; region: string;
  requestCount: number; bytesTransferred: number;
}

// User tiers
type UserTier = 'free' | 'pro' | 'team' | 'enterprise';
```
